channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: RECALL: Walmart warns dressers could fall over
item.description: Walmart has issued a recall for a product that the United States Consumer Product Safety Commission says poses a "risk of serious injury or death.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Ex-LAPD officer accused of killing unarmed homeless man in 2015 arrested at LAX
item.description: Authorities arrested a former Los Angeles Police Department officer suspected of killing an unarmed homeless man in 2015, the department confirmed Friday.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, NewsNation

item.title: Indiana University fires student newspaper adviser who refused to block news stories
item.description: Tension between Indiana University and its student newspaper flared this week with the elimination of the outlet's print editions and the firing of a faculty adviser, who refused an order to keep news stories out of a homecoming edition.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Organized crime rings drive street takeovers by using kids to steal cars for wild stunts: expert
item.description: Street takeovers organized on social media involve stolen high-performance cars, with thieves targeting luxury vehicles and using new technology to steal them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Evacuees detail harrowing scenes of flooding in coastal Alaska villages as airlift continues
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The house rocked as if an earthquake had struck, and suddenly it was floating. Water seeped in through the front door, and waves smacked the big glass window.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dressers sold on Amazon recalled due to tip-over hazard
item.description: 76,500 dressers sold on Amazon are being recalled due to the risk of injury or death from a tip-over hazard.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, NewsNation

item.title: Ace Frehley, Kiss' original lead guitarist and founding member, dies at 74
item.description: Ace Frehley, the original lead guitarist and founding member of the glam rock band Kiss, who captivated audiences with his elaborate galactic makeup and smoking guitar, died Thursday. He was 74.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: State attorneys general sue Trump administration for canceled solar program funding
item.description: The attorneys general of more than a dozen states on Thursday sued the Trump administration over the termination of $7 billion in funding intended for affordable solar energy projects across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil can freely travel around US as he fights his deportation case, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has lifted travel restrictions for Mahmoud Khalil, allowing the Palestinian activist to speak at rallies and other events across the U.S. as he fights his deportation case brought by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Capital makeover: Trump shows off plans for giant arch in DC
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday detailed plans for an arch to be built near the Lincoln Memorial at the entrance to Washington, D.C., the latest instance of Trump's efforts to give the capital a makeover.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 9- and 10-year-old charged with attempted murder, rape of 5-year-old
item.description: The Cuyahoga County Prosecutor’s Office announced charges against two juveniles following an assault on a 5-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Aldi starts Thanksgiving meal price war: $40 for 10 people
item.description: Aldi is selling “21 total products and ingredients” that the buyer prepares for this year’s Turkey Day.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family members share Diane Keaton's cause of death
item.description: Family members of legendary actress Diane Keaton, who passed away Saturday at the age of 79, revealed the Oscar-winning actress died during a battle with pneumonia, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Latest: Federal immigration officers in Chicago area required to wear body cameras, judge says
item.description: Federal immigration officers in the Chicago area will be required to wear body cameras, U.S. District Judge Sara Ellis said Thursday after seeing tear gas and other aggressive steps used against protesters. Community efforts to oppose U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: MI5 chief says China is a security threat to UK as officials trade blame over spy case collapse
item.description: LONDON (AP) — China poses a daily threat to Britain’s security, the head of the country's domestic intelligence agency said Thursday, remarks that step up pressure on authorities to explain why the prosecution of two men charged with spying for Beijing collapsed just before they were due to stand tr
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Food assistance is safe through October, but it may be at risk if the shutdown continues
item.description: A federal program that provides food assistance to 40 million low-income people could be at risk in November if the government shutdown isn't resolved by then. And in at least some places, new applications for the program are not being approved.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge orders federal agents in Chicago area to wear body cameras after violent clashes
item.description: U.S. District Judge Sara Ellis has ordered federal immigration officers in the Chicago area to wear body cameras after seeing aggressive steps used against protesters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Food aid is safe this month, but shutdown could put it at risk in November
item.description: A federal program that provides food assistance to 40 million low-income people could be at risk in November if the government shutdown isn't resolved by then.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Family members share Diane Keaton's cause of death
item.description: Family members of legendary actress Diane Keaton, who passed away Saturday at the age of 79, revealed the Oscar-winning actress died during a battle with pneumonia, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Florida teen arrested after shooting himself in staged abduction, deputies say
item.description: The 17-year-old who staged his own abduction and caused a statewide AMBER Alert has been arrested, Florida deputies say.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, ABC 4

item.title: Smoothie straw leads to arrest in 1984 cold case murder of New York teen
item.description: A straw from a smoothie cup was the evidence that led police in New York to the man they suspect of killing a 16-year-old girl on Long Island in 1984, the Nassau County District Attorney’s Office announced on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, ABC 4

item.title: Smucker sues Trader Joe's over alleged 'dupe' Uncrustables product
item.description: The lawsuit also acknowledged Trader Joe’s use of blue packaging is similar to Smucker’s trademarked branding.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, Fox 5

item.title: Alaska airlifting hundreds from storm-devastated coastal villages
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — One of the most significant airlifts in Alaska history was underway Wednesday to move hundreds of people from coastal villages ravaged by high surf and strong winds from the remnants of Typhoon Halong last weekend, officials said.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks the Trump administration from firing workers during the government shutdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s administration from firing workers during the government shutdown, saying the cuts appeared to be politically motivated and were being carried out without much thought. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Journalists turn in access badges, exit Pentagon rather than agree to new reporting rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dozens of reporters turned in access badges and exited the Pentagon on Wednesday rather than agree to government-imposed restrictions on their work, pushing journalists who cover the American military further from the seat of its power.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Journalists turn in access badges, exit Pentagon rather than agree to new reporting rules
item.description: Dozens of reporters turned in access badges and exited the Pentagon on Wednesday rather than agree to government-imposed restrictions on their work, pushing journalists who cover the American military further from the seat of its power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump shutdown layoffs
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday blocked President Trump’s efforts to lay off thousands of federal employees amid the government shutdown, for now. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Young Republicans call for immediate resignations after report on GOP group chat
item.description: The chat also featured racial slurs and reportedly said that rape was “epic,” according to the report.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: State Department revokes 6 visas over Charlie Kirk comments
item.description: The six individuals were identified as from Argentina, South Africa, Mexico, Brazil, Germany and Paraguay.
item.dc:creator: Meg Hilling, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 17th century book found in university donation bin
item.description: The people who help run the Michigan State University Surplus Store come across thousands of donated books each month but nothing like one donated last year.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, WGN 9

item.title: Popular rice brand issues nationwide recall: FDA
item.description: A popular brand of rice has been recalled after it was reported that there could be small stones in the product.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: GOP sets the stage for extended shutdown
item.description: Republicans and the Trump administration are bracing for a lengthy government shutdown, taking steps to alleviate any political pain that might boomerang on them while seeking to make life difficult for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Katie Porter expresses regret for tense exchange with reporter, berating staffer
item.description: Former California congresswoman Katie Porter is attempting to press "reset" on her campaign for governor following a disastrous week that started with a video of a tense exchange with a journalist and then evolved into a broader critique of her temperament when another video surfaced.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: Trump and budget chief Vought are making this a government shutdown unlike any other
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is making this government shutdown unlike any the nation has ever seen, giving his budget office rare authority to pick winners and losers — who gets paid or fired, which programs are cut or survive — in an unprecedented restructuring across the federal workf
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Millions of taxpayers face Oct. 15 tax deadline, despite government shutdown: IRS
item.description: (NEXSTAR) – An estimated 20 million taxpayers will need to file before this week's deadline, the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) warned Tuesday. While most people filed earlier this year, returns are due by midnight on Wednesday for taxpayers who filed an extension for tax year 2024.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Carlos Santana hits back at rumor he opposed Bad Bunny Super Bowl show
item.description: "AI has accelerated the spread of misinformation, making it harder for the truth to break through the noise.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, Fox 5
